message:
[HIGH] fcp-google-discovery/auth: redact secrets in Debug across the auth-source surface

Five public types in the Google auth path — GoogleRefreshExchangeRequest,
GoogleOAuthRefreshSource, GoogleAuthSource, GoogleMaterializedAuth, and
the chain that includes them — derived Debug, leaving the access_token,
refresh_token, and OAuth client_secret reachable through any
`tracing::debug!(?selection)` or `format!("{:?}", source)` call site.

No active production caller logs these structs today, so the leak is
latent — but the same shape was hardened in fcp-oauth/token.rs (1fcd949)
specifically because Debug-via-derive on secret-bearing structs is the
default path future call sites take.

This commit replaces the auto-derived Debug on the four secret-bearing
types with manual impls that emit `[REDACTED]` for client_secret,
refresh_token, and access_token while preserving non-secret fields
(client_id, token_url, requested_scopes, granted_scopes, source kind,
quota_project_id) for diagnostics. Five regression tests — one per
struct/variant — assert the secret strings do not appear in `{:?}`
output and `[REDACTED]` does.

Audit context: surveyed gmail/google-drive/google-calendar plus the
shared discovery layer for scope over-request, token-zone binding, and
refresh-token leak. Scope handling is narrow-by-default with explicit
escalation triggers (provisioning.rs:115-149, validates each trigger
against declared escalation_paths or returns UnknownEscalationTrigger);
this audit found no over-request path. The latent Debug leak above is
the highest-severity finding.

Build-status note: the test changes were authored from code reading;
the local cargo target is contended in this session and `cargo test`
did not complete in-process. The Debug impls are mechanically
straightforward and follow the established fcp-oauth/token.rs pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
